_description: FQL Strategy Registry — institutional memory of all strategy ideas, tested candidates, and deployed strategies
_purpose: Preserves research knowledge, enables crossbreeding, feeds Strategy Factory

strategies.rule_summary: Price above VWAP on longer timeframe, trend continuation entry with ATR trailing stop
strategies.notes: Highest trade count (41.7%). Layer 1 backbone. Regime gate: avoid HIGH_VOL (+$1,166 recovery queued)

strategies.rule_summary: Pullback after EMA crossover with timestop exit. Crossbred from PB entry + EMA filter + time exit
strategies.notes: Only MES strategy. Regime gate: avoid LOW_RV (+$246 recovery queued)

strategies.rule_summary: First 30-min opening range breakout with VWAP + volume + momentum confirmation
strategies.notes: Morning session (10:00-12:00 ET). Regime gate: avoid LOW_VOL (+$777 recovery queued)

strategies.rule_summary: Donchian channel breakout (50-100 bar extreme) with GRINDING persistence filter and profit ladder exit
strategies.notes: True trend-follower (60-bar hold). GRINDING-only filter. Regime gate: avoid HIGH_RV (+$2,199 recovery queued). Needs 150 trades for promotion.

strategies.rule_summary: Bollinger Band equilibrium bounce in trending sessions. Gold mean-reverts cleanly intraday.
strategies.notes: 3x efficiency (5.6% trades, 16.6% PnL). Layer 3 tail engine. Regime gate: avoid HIGH_RV (+$350 recovery queued)

strategies.rule_summary: Pullback during downtrend on 5m bars, 6-layer filter stack (trend + ADX + volume + VWAP + regime + EMA)
strategies.notes: Deepest filter stack (6 layers). Very rare trades. Requires HIGH_RV + TRENDING.

strategies.rule_summary: VWAP cross + 2x volume surge + RSI confirm + EMA slope. M2K-specific momentum burst.
strategies.notes: Was 9.0/10 on 2yr, collapsed to 6.0/10 on 6.7yr. Edge narrower than expected. Needs 50 trades for promotion.

strategies.rule_summary: Session VWAP deviation band fade (2 sigma + RSI). Targets close session 15:00-15:55.
strategies.notes: Close stabilizer. 100% param stability on 6.7yr. M2K-specific. Needs 60 trades for promotion.

strategies.rule_summary: BB(20,2.0) inside KC(20,1.5x ATR) for 12+ bars → first expansion bar + momentum direction. Profit ladder exit.
strategies.notes: First vol expansion strategy. Longer squeeze (12 bars) = better tail profile. M2K-specific. Needs 100 trades for promotion.

strategies.rule_summary: Enhanced ORB with volume + range filters on M2K
strategies.notes: 8.0/10 on 2yr data. 100% param stability. Needs extended-history validation and 150+ trade accumulation.

strategies.rule_summary: VWAP deviation mean reversion on crude oil micro
strategies.notes: 6.5/10 on 2yr. 70% param stability (weakest of probation). Needs extended validation and 150+ trades.

strategies.rule_summary: VWAP deviation band fade on MNQ close session
strategies.notes: REJECTED — edge illusory on 6.7yr data. Was 8.0/10 on 2yr. PF collapsed from 1.80 to 0.85. Do not resurrect.
strategies.rejection_description: Backtest profitable but walk-forward collapses

strategies.rule_summary: TTM Squeeze on MNQ short side
strategies.notes: REJECTED — framework doesn't work on MNQ. No viable edge found.
strategies.rejection_description: Strategy loses money across all modes and parameter variants

strategies.rule_summary: TTM Squeeze on MGC long side
strategies.notes: REJECTED — framework doesn't work on MGC long side.
strategies.rejection_description: Strategy loses money across all modes and parameter variants

strategies.rule_summary: Session sweep detection → bias determination → pullback entry. State machine pattern.
strategies.notes: REJECTED — PF < 1.0 on ALL assets/modes. Sweep detection too noisy on 5m bars. Fixed stops don't scale. Salvageable: state machine pattern, sweep detection as filter component.
strategies.rejection_description: ICT-derived concepts have not produced systematic edge in FQL

strategies.rule_summary: Liquidity sweeper — same sweep framework as ICT-010
strategies.notes: REJECTED — same sweep detection issues as ICT-010.
strategies.rejection_description: ICT-derived concepts have not produced systematic edge in FQL

strategies.rule_summary: ATR(fast)/ATR(slow) < threshold (dead zone) → breakout with ATR expansion confirmation
strategies.notes: REJECTED — tested at thresholds 0.45-0.75. No viable edge on any asset. PF degrades as threshold tightens. Sensitivity analysis confirmed structural lack of edge.
strategies.rejection_description: Strategy loses money across all modes and parameter variants

strategies.rule_summary: IBS = (Close-Low)/(High-Low). Buy next open when IBS < 0.20, sell when IBS > 0.80. Close at end of next day.
strategies.notes: 78% documented win rate on broad indices. Russell thin liquidity amplifies overshoots. DAILY timeframe — different from 5m intraday. Tier 1 candidate.

strategies.rule_summary: Enhancement to ORB-MGC-Long: skip entries when VIX > 25, widen targets when VIX 15-25
strategies.notes: Enhancement, not standalone strategy. Needs VIX data feed. Tier 1 candidate.

strategies.rule_summary: Narrowest 5m bar in last 7 → bracket order above/below high/low → first triggered = entry. 2x ATR stop, EOD exit.
strategies.notes: Classic Crabel contraction signal. Pairs well with squeeze strategies. Tier 2 candidate.

strategies.rule_summary: Overnight gap > threshold → fade toward fill. 76% of RTY gaps fill during RTH. Overnight midpoint predicts direction (76%).
strategies.notes: 1 trade/day max. Structural edge from institutional rebalancing. Tier 2 candidate.

strategies.rule_summary: Buy at 2 PM day before FOMC, sell 15 min before announcement. No stop or wide 3x ATR.
strategies.notes: REJECTED — MES PF=0.72 (negative), MNQ PF=1.19 with only 53 trades. Edge has weakened as literature warned (post-2016). Not viable for intraday simulation (overnight hold not supported).
strategies.rejection_description: Too few trades to determine edge (< 20)

strategies.rule_summary: BBW percentile < 5% → wait for BBWP to cross above 13-bar SMA → enter momentum direction. Trail with ATR, take profit when BBWP > 80%.
strategies.notes: Most selective squeeze filter — fires very rarely, largest asymmetric payoffs. Tier 2 candidate.

strategies.rule_summary: Range = MAX(HH-LC, HC-LL) over N days → Long = Open + K1*Range, Short = Open - K2*Range
strategies.notes: Widely used by CTAs. Fires too frequently for intraday. Better as daily overlay. Tier 3.

strategies.rule_summary: Multi-contraction pattern (each pullback smaller than previous) → breakout on expansion
strategies.notes: Complex pattern detection. Gold forms cleaner VCPs than equity indices. Rare on 5m. Tier 3.

strategies.rule_summary: Not a strategy — controller enhancement. Pause strategies during event windows, widen stops, reduce size.
strategies.notes: Implement as strategy_controller enhancement after freeze lifts. Tier 3.

strategies.rule_summary: Buy close first trading day after June 23rd, sell first trading day of July. 1.34% avg gain over 6 days.
strategies.notes: 1-2 trades/year. Consistent since 1988. Now semi-annual (Dec added). Very low priority. Tier 3.

strategies.rule_summary: Detect tight consolidation range → breakout with volume confirmation → enter in breakout direction
strategies.notes: User-mentioned. Similar to NR7/TTM Squeeze concept. Needs strict rule definition before testing.

strategies.rule_summary: Detect liquidity pools (swing highs/lows) → wait for stop-run sweep → enter reversal
strategies.notes: User-mentioned. ICT-style but different from failed ICT-010/011. Key difference: focus on the reversal after sweep, not the sweep itself. Needs mechanical rule translation.

strategies.rule_summary: ICT liquidity sweep — improved version with better sweep detection and entry timing
strategies.notes: User-mentioned. Must fix ICT-010/011 flaws: use ATR-scaled stops (not fixed), improve sweep detection (require volume confirmation), add regime filter. Salvage state machine from ICT-010.

strategies.rule_summary: Rolling VWAP mean reversion — fade extreme deviations from rolling VWAP
strategies.notes: REJECTED — failed on all 10 combos. Best PF=1.17 MNQ-long.
strategies.rejection_description: Strategy loses money across all modes and parameter variants

strategies.rule_summary: VIX-derived implied move channel. Session-based, 1 trade/day. NY VIX Channel Trend (exlux).
strategies.notes: REJECTED — failed on all 6 combos. Realized vol proxy insufficient.
strategies.rejection_description: Too few trades to determine edge (< 20)

strategies.rule_summary: Compression → expansion with EMA 150 trend filter. Hybrid volatility breakout.
strategies.notes: REJECTED — failed on all 6 combos. Best PF=1.04.
strategies.rejection_description: PF 0.9-1.1 — near breakeven, not enough to overcome costs

strategies.rule_summary: Classic BB/KC squeeze with SAR trailing filter. LazyBear implementation.
strategies.notes: REJECTED — failed on all 10 combos. Best PF=1.02.
strategies.rejection_description: PF 0.9-1.1 — near breakeven, not enough to overcome costs

strategies.rule_summary: RSI(2) < 10 on 5m bars → buy next bar. Russell thin book creates overshoots that snap back.
strategies.notes: M2K-specific microstructure edge. Russell 2000 has documented RSI(2) oversold bounce. Quick to test.

strategies.rule_summary: Historical volatility percentile < 10th → wait for expansion above 25th → enter momentum direction
strategies.notes: Alternative vol expansion trigger. Different from BB/KC squeeze — uses realized vol percentile instead.

strategies.rule_summary: Donchian breakout on extreme lookback (100+ bars). Enter only on strongest breakouts with volume.
strategies.notes: Extension of Donchian-MNQ concept. May overlap — check correlation. Different assets could work.

strategies.rule_summary: Initial Balance (first 30-60min) breakout on gold. Gold IB breaks tend to trend strongly.
strategies.notes: Similar to ORB but uses market profile IB concept. Could overlap with ORB-MGC-Long — check.

strategies.rule_summary: Fade session VWAP extreme deviations during afternoon session
strategies.notes: REJECTED — failed research gate on all 10 asset/direction combos. Best PF=1.08.
strategies.rejection_description: Strategy loses money across all modes and parameter variants

strategies.rule_summary: Gold session-based mean reversion targeting afternoon session
strategies.notes: REJECTED — failed on MGC both directions.
strategies.rejection_description: Strategy loses money across all modes and parameter variants

strategies.rule_summary: Close session liquidity sweep with reversal entry
strategies.notes: REJECTED — failed on all 6 combos. Best PF=1.07.
strategies.rejection_description: PF 0.9-1.1 — near breakeven, not enough to overcome costs

strategies.rule_summary: Close session momentum continuation play
strategies.notes: REJECTED — zero trades on all 8 combos. Strategy logic incompatible with data.
strategies.rejection_description: Code bug found — hypothesis not tested, may revisit

strategies.rule_summary: Gap momentum system — trade in direction of opening gap with momentum confirmation
strategies.notes: PROMOTED to probation 2026-03-15. MGC-long: PF=1.72, WF 6.7/10. MNQ-long: PF=1.31, WF 6.7/10 (83% window survival). Best combo: MGC-long.

strategies.rule_summary: Range expansion detection → momentum entry
strategies.notes: PROMOTED to probation 2026-03-15. MCL-short: PF=1.46, Sharpe=2.39, 214 trades. WF 10/10 PERFECT. 75% window survival, 100% param stability. First MCL strategy.
strategies.kill_details: Half-life monitor: decay=0.65 ARCHIVE_CANDIDATE. Sharpe 2.39->0.46 (1yr)->-0.13 (6mo). Edge rapidly decaying despite WF 10/10.

strategies.rule_summary: Natural gas mean reversion — NG has strong seasonal and inventory-driven mean reversion patterns
strategies.notes: Asset expansion candidate. NG has different microstructure from oil. Needs data acquisition first.

strategies.rule_summary: Dow micro-futures trend following — MYM has slower, more deliberate trends than MNQ
strategies.notes: Asset expansion candidate. MYM data already available. Similar to MNQ but slower — may find distinct edge.

strategies.rule_summary: Crude oil micro-futures ORB/breakout strategy — oil has strong inventory-driven breakout patterns
strategies.notes: Asset expansion candidate. MCL data available. Oil has different liquidity profile from equity/gold.

strategies.rule_summary: At 15:30 ET, compute return from prev close. If positive go long, negative go short. Exit 15:59. Filter: only when VIX > 20.
strategies.notes: REJECTED — no viable edge on any asset/direction. Best: M2K-both PF=1.05. ATR proxy insufficient for VIX filter. Academic edge does not translate to 5m micro-futures without real VIX data.
strategies.rejection_description: Strategy loses money across all modes and parameter variants

strategies.rule_summary: Compute noise boundary = Open * (1 +/- avg abs intraday return * time-of-day scale). Break above upper boundary -> long. Trail stop below VWAP. Flat EOD.
strategies.notes: PROMOTED to probation 2026-03-15. WF 10/10 PERFECT. MNQ-long: PF=1.28, Sharpe=1.40, 609 trades. 83% window survival, cross-asset edge on MES+MYM, 100% param stability. Academic source: Zarattini/Aziz/Barbon 2024.
strategies.kill_details: Correlated with Donchian-MNQ-Long (corr=0.374, same MNQ/long/breakout). NoiseBoundary is stronger — keep this, archive Donchian.

strategies.rule_summary: Price > VWAP AND 9 EMA > 20 EMA. Wait for pullback to touch VWAP from above with bullish close. Enter long. Stop 1.5x ATR below. Target prev HOD or 2x ATR.
strategies.notes: VWAP pullback is documented as highest-probability trade on ES. Multiple practitioner sources. ~100-150 trades/yr. Fills MES-long gap.

strategies.rule_summary: Mark high/low of first 15min (09:30-09:45). Long on 5m close above 15min high. Stop = 15min low. Target = 2x range above breakout. Flat by 15:00.
strategies.notes: REJECTED 2026-03-15. M2K-long PF=0.75, Sharpe=-1.55, WR=26.1%. Negative edge. No viable combo across 5 assets. Simple ORB crowded out on equity indexes.
strategies.rejection_description: Strategy loses money across all modes and parameter variants

strategies.rule_summary: KC(20 EMA, 2.0x ATR10). Long when 5m closes above upper KC band. Confirm RSI(14)>50 AND ADX(14)>25. Stop = KC midline. Target = 1.5x band width above upper.
strategies.notes: REJECTED 2026-03-15. M2K-long PF=1.02, Sharpe=0.10, WR=38.5%. Near-zero edge despite 1129 trades. No viable cross-asset combo. 77% WR claim not replicable on 5m futures.
strategies.rejection_description: PF 0.9-1.1 — near breakeven, not enough to overcome costs

strategies.rule_summary: Rolling z-score of MES/M2K ratio (100 bars). When z > +2.0 (M2K underperforming): long M2K. Exit when z < +1.0. Stop at z > +3.0. Flat EOD.
strategies.notes: QuantStart confirmed cointegration on 1m bars 2007-2014. Structural mean reversion between large/small cap. ~40-60 trades/yr. Fills M2K-long gap. Needs both MES and M2K data simultaneously.

strategies.rule_summary: Confirm uptrend (HH last 30min + 9EMA > 21EMA). Wait for pullback to VWAP. Long on bullish 5m close above VWAP. Stop 1.5x ATR below. Target prev HOD.
strategies.notes: REJECTED 2026-03-15. MCL-long PF=1.08, Sharpe=0.51, WR=48.3%. Marginal edge below gate. No viable cross-asset combo. VWAP bounce on CL needs tighter discretionary filtering.
strategies.rejection_description: PF 0.9-1.1 — near breakeven, not enough to overcome costs

strategies.rule_summary: Mark high/low of first 15min after 09:00 ET. Long on 5m close above 15min high. Stop = 15min low. Target = 2x range. Flat by 14:00. Avoid EIA day Wed 10:30.
strategies.notes: REJECTED 2026-03-15. MCL-long PF=0.96, Sharpe=-0.23, WR=45.1%. Negative edge. No viable cross-asset combo. Academic ORB edge on crude may be timeframe/period specific.
strategies.rejection_description: PF 0.9-1.1 — near breakeven, not enough to overcome costs

strategies.rule_summary: Close all by 10:25 ET Wed. After EIA 10:30, wait 5min. If draw > 5M bbl vs expected: long. If build > 5M bbl: short. Stop 1.5x ATR. Target 2x stop. Flat by 14:00.
strategies.notes: Wednesday 10:30 ET is highest-volatility recurring event for crude. ~20-25 trades/yr (only outsized surprises). Fills event-driven gap + MCL coverage.

strategies.rule_summary: Enter long Monday open of OPEX week (3rd week of month). Exit Friday close. No intraday stop. Driven by gamma hedging and delta unwind.
strategies.notes: Quantpedia + QuantifiedStrategies. SPY avg +0.2% per OPEX week. 12 trades/yr. Effect has weakened last 3 years. Fills event-driven gap. Low priority due to weakening edge.

strategies.rule_summary: EMA200 trend + EMA50 pullback bounce + RSI confirmation. US session only (08-17 ET). Asia/Europe filtered out.
strategies.notes: CONDITIONAL_PASS (5/7). First validated non-equity strategy. PF 1.58, Sharpe 2.92, 43 trades US-session. Walk-forward 1.76/1.45. Parameter stability 80%. 6E cross-validates PF 1.72. Failures: Bootstrap CI (sample size) and MC ruin (threshold proximity). Restrictions: US session only, long only, MICRO/REDUCED tier, review after 30+ additional forward trades. POST-PROBATION ENHANCEMENT: Carry bias filter (60d return proxy) improves PF 1.58->1.67, Sharpe 2.92->3.25. NOT applied during probation to preserve clean forward evidence. Apply at promotion if strategy passes Week 8 review.

strategies.rule_summary: Asian range breakout at London open (03:00 ET). Short only. ATR filter + volume confirmation + trailing stop.
strategies.notes: CONDITIONAL_PASS (5/8). Walk-forward most stable of expansion (1.08/1.35). 125 trades, PF 1.20, Sharpe 1.09. Short bias (short PF 1.21, long PF 1.00). Regime-stable (0 catastrophic cells, trades all regimes). Param stability 75% (ATR=30 variants PF 1.46-1.54). Asset-specific to 6J (6E PF 1.00, 6B PF 0.66). Complementary to MomPB-6J: different session (London vs US), different direction (short bias vs long only), different entry (range breakout vs pullback). Restrictions: London session, MICRO tier, both directions, review after 50+ forward trades. POST-PROBATION ENHANCEMENT: Carry bias filter improves PF 1.20->1.36, Sharpe 1.09->2.03. Biggest improvement of any strategy. NOT applied during probation. Apply at promotion.

strategies.rule_summary: 6E session breakout needs refinement. PF 1.07/1.08 directional splits. Walk-forward H2 improving.
6B session breakout: NO_EDGE. PF 0.55-0.85. Pound requires different native logic.
strategies.rejection_description: Strategy logic does not transfer to this asset class

strategies.rule_summary: EMA200 trend + EMA50 pullback bounce + RSI confirmation. US session only. Surfaced by batch_first_pass cross-asset scan.
strategies.notes: Batch discovery: PF 1.72, Sharpe 2.90, 48 trades, US session. Previously missed because 6E was only tested without session filter. Needs validation battery.

strategies.rule_summary: 50-day EMA trend direction + 20-day Donchian breakout. Daily bars resampled from 5m. ATR trailing stop. Long only.
strategies.notes: PASS (7/8). First multi-horizon strategy. First PASS-level expansion result. PF 3.65, Sharpe 5.13, 44 trades. Bootstrap CI lower bound 1.38 (above 1.0). Parameter stability 100% (PF 2.63-3.96 across all combos). Top-trade removal PF 2.85. Walk-forward H1=1.14, H2=5.62. Only failure: MC ruin at 2K (sample size, not strategy defect). Restrictions: long only, daily bars, REDUCED tier, review after 15+ forward trades. CONVERGENT CONFIRMATION: DualThrust Daily MGC long PF 2.79 (independent entry logic, same edge). Two different systems confirm gold daily long momentum.

strategies.rule_summary: When a 5m bullish FVG gets body closed below (inverted), short. When bearish FVG gets body closed above, long. Higher-TF trend filter (4H EMA). Target 1:1 R:R or next session liquidity. Extracted from full ICT mech model — only the mechanical inversion component.
strategies.notes: Compact hypothesis extracted from discretionary ICT model. Full model too discretionary for FQL. ICT family previously failed (ICT-010, ICT-011). This is a one-shot factory test to formally close or validate the ICT family. LOW PRIORITY — defer until higher-value candidates are processed.

strategies.rule_summary: DualThrust range breakout, daily bars, short only. First rates directional signal. PF 1.48, 31 trades. Bonds down = rates rising = macro tightening.
strategies.notes: MONITOR ONLY. Too few trades (31) for any conclusion. First positive rates signal found. Revisit when more data accumulates.

strategies.rule_summary: Monthly ROC + SMA trend rules on 2Y Treasury futures capturing FOMC tightening/easing cycle persistence. Proxy: ZF (5Y). Source: Quantpedia/Dujava.
strategies.notes: ACCEPTED via harvest review. Fills EVENT + CARRY factor gaps. Blocked by: no TU data (using ZF proxy), monthly horizon needs infrastructure, insufficient history for validation. Revisit after rates data backfill.

strategies.rule_summary: Rank currencies by interest rate, long highest-yield, short lowest-yield. Monthly rebalance. Classic carry with crash-risk filters needed. Source: Quantpedia.
strategies.notes: ACCEPTED via harvest review. Pure CARRY factor fill. Blocked by: only 3 FX pairs, no rate data feed, monthly horizon, crash risk unfiltered. Best near-term use: directional bias filter for existing 6J/6E strategies rather than standalone.

strategies.rule_summary: Rank currencies by yield-curve slope, trade cross-currency flattening. 200-year evidence, Sharpe ~2x carry. Source: Quantpedia/Chen.
strategies.notes: ACCEPTED, LOW priority. More sophisticated CARRY than simple rate differential but heavily blocked: needs curve data for multiple countries, cross-currency ranking needs 6-10 pairs, complex implementation. Revisit after plain carry is tested.

strategies.rule_summary: Rank currencies by PPP valuation vs USD. Long undervalued, short overvalued. Quarterly. Source: Quantpedia.
strategies.notes: ACCEPTED, LOW priority. Value factor (not momentum). Blocked by: needs OECD PPP + CPI data, quarterly horizon, only 3 FX pairs. Less actionable than carry or event-driven.

strategies.rule_summary: 12-month time-series momentum on Treasury futures. Long when 12m return positive, short when negative. Monthly rebalance. Source: OpenClaw note 07.
strategies.notes: MONITOR. Rates TSM mechanism appears real but sample too thin. Short-only edge: ZN PF 3.89 (6 trades), ZF PF 4.13 (5 trades), ZB PF 4.10 (7 trades). Long side catastrophic on all contracts (PF 0.00-0.44). Dominated by 2022-2024 tightening cycle — one regime drives everything. 12M and 6M lookbacks produce identical results. Not ready for probation. Needs 10+ year data covering 2+ full rate cycles. Do not iterate without longer history.

strategies.rule_summary: Daily Donchian breakout trend system on FX futures. Source: OpenClaw note 10.
strategies.notes: ACCEPTED. Conversion priority #2. FX daily-bar breakout. Passes momentum high-bar: daily horizon on FX (existing FX strategies are intraday). Similar to fx_daily_trend but different entry (Donchian vs EMA+Donchian).

strategies.rule_summary: Asian session range breakout into London open on FX futures. Source: OpenClaw note 08.
strategies.notes: ACCEPTED. Conversion priority #3. STRUCTURAL factor — session-transition edge. Similar to FXBreak-6J but different framing (Asian range vs Tokyo-specific). May overlap with existing session breakout.

strategies.rule_summary: FX futures opening range breakout. Source: OpenClaw note 09.
strategies.notes: ACCEPTED. Conversion priority #4. STRUCTURAL — ORB adapted to FX session structure. Lower priority because FQL already has ORB family and FX session breakout. Value depends on how different FX ORB is from existing.

strategies.rule_summary: Treasury curve drift mean-reversion basket. Trade ZN vs ZB (or ZF vs ZN) spread based on curve steepening/flattening signals. Source: OpenClaw note 06.
strategies.notes: ACCEPTED but BLOCKED. Conversion priority #5. Rates-native relative value — genuinely different factor (not momentum). Blocked by: needs curve hedge implementation, spread trading infrastructure, and deeper rates data.

strategies.rule_summary: Pre-FOMC announcement drift: equity indices tend to drift higher in the 24 hours before FOMC announcements. Long MES/MNQ day before FOMC, exit after announcement. ~8 events/year.
strategies.notes: PASS (7/8). First EVENT factor strategy. PF 1.73, 96 trades, Sharpe 3.85. Momentum correlation: -0.004 (ZERO — proven independent). Walk-forward: H1=2.03, H2=1.46. Top-trade removal: PF 1.49/1.27/1.15. MC ruin 4.2%. MES cross-validates PF 1.27. 2023 bad year explained: Fed pause reduced anticipation drift. Restrictions: MNQ primary, MICRO tier, 8 trades/yr, review after 8+ forward trades.

strategies.rule_summary: Treasury futures tend to rally on inflation announcement days (CPI/PPI). Long ZN/ZF on CPI release day. ~12 events/year.
strategies.notes: MONITOR. Original long-bias hypothesis REJECTED for current sample. CPI-day rates effect appears real but directionally REGIME-DEPENDENT. ZB short PF 2.81 (19 trades, 63% WR) is the standout — hawkish regime causes bond selloffs on CPI. ZN short PF 1.34 moderate. ZF long PF 1.24 weak. Not ready for probation. Needs rates data backfill ($12.59) to test across tightening + easing regimes and determine whether this is a regime-switchable strategy or period-specific artifact. Do not iterate further without backfill.

strategies.rule_summary: Equity indices tend to rise during monthly options expiration week. Long MES/MNQ Monday of OPEX week, exit Friday. ~12 events/year. Source: Quantpedia/QuantifiedStrategies.
strategies.notes: ACCEPTED. EVENT conversion priority #3. Calendar-driven weekly hold. Different mechanism from FOMC (dealer hedging/gamma exposure vs Fed anticipation). 12 trades/year — needs multi-year data for validation.

strategies.rule_summary: Equity index directional bias after CPI release based on surprise direction. Trade the post-announcement momentum. ~12 events/year.
strategies.notes: ACCEPTED but BLOCKED. EVENT conversion priority #4. Blocked by: only ~24 events in current data (2yr), needs regime check to distinguish inflationary vs disinflationary market reactions. Revisit after data backfill.

strategies.rule_summary: Trade the 30-second price box formed immediately after EIA inventory release on crude oil. Breakout of the initial reaction range. ~48 events/year.
strategies.notes: ACCEPTED but BLOCKED. EVENT conversion priority #5. Blocked by: needs sub-minute execution model (30-second box on 5m bars is too coarse), needs high-resolution data or tick-level timing. Note: simpler EIA reaction (note EIA-Reaction) already tested and rejected at PF 0.93 on 5m bars.

strategies.rule_summary: Equity index overnight drift: buy at close, sell at next open. Captures the well-documented overnight equity premium. ~250 events/year.
strategies.notes: MONITOR. Overnight premium exists (MNQ PF 1.09, 1641 trades) but too weak/inconsistent for probation. Bear market vulnerability: 2022 PF 0.82 (-$4.9K), 2026 YTD PF 0.60 (-$3.1K). MaxDD $7,210 — larger than any current probation strategy. Do not optimize further. Revisit only if a regime filter can be added without making it disguised momentum.

strategies.rule_summary: Trade equity index futures based on closing auction order imbalance flow. Institutional rebalancing creates predictable close dynamics.
strategies.notes: ACCEPTED but BLOCKED. STRUCTURAL priority #2. Blocked by: needs auction imbalance data feed (not available in standard OHLCV). Would need Level 2 or MOC imbalance data.

strategies.rule_summary: Trade Treasury futures around quarterly roll dates exploiting spread compression/expansion patterns during contract transition.
strategies.notes: ACCEPTED but BLOCKED. STRUCTURAL priority #3. Rates-native structural edge — roll microstructure is genuinely different from directional trading. Blocked by: needs spread-level data between front and back month, and roll date calendar.

strategies.rule_summary: Trade crude oil calendar spread structure (contango/backwardation) around roll periods. Exploits WTI term structure dynamics.
strategies.notes: ACCEPTED but BLOCKED. STRUCTURAL priority #4. Energy-native structural edge. Blocked by: needs multi-contract roll expression (front vs back month spread), not available from single continuous contract.

strategies.rule_summary: Gold overnight drift: buy at US close, sell at next US open. Similar to equity overnight premium but on gold — captures Asian/European session flow into gold.
strategies.notes: ACCEPTED but BLOCKED. STRUCTURAL priority #5. Blocked by: source likely references spot gold or ETF — needs validation that the effect exists on MGC futures specifically. May not transfer cleanly from spot to futures due to roll and carry.

strategies.rule_summary: Trade FOMC rate decision cycles based on rate change direction patterns. Source: TradingView public script.
strategies.notes: ACCEPTED. TV EVENT batch priority #1. Complements PreFOMC-Drift with a different FOMC angle (decision cycle vs pre-announcement drift).

strategies.rule_summary: Trade off NFP release high/low levels as support/resistance. First Friday monthly. Source: TradingView public script.
strategies.notes: CONDITIONAL_PASS (7/9). Second EVENT strategy. First successful salvage. PF 1.66, Sharpe 3.25, 65 trades, WR 42%. Long-only (short side PF 0.38 removed via salvage). NFP-specific: 27x avg PnL vs generic equity long. Momentum corr 0.065 (independent). Walk-forward: H1=1.50, H2=1.61. Top-trade robust: PF 1.54/1.44/1.33. MES cross-validates PF 1.48. WATCH ITEMS: T3 weakening (PF 1.09), MaxDD ,431 (multi-day hold structural), Bootstrap CI 0.93 (below 1.0). Restrictions: MNQ primary, long only, MICRO tier, review after 8+ forward trades.
watch_items: T3 walk-forward weakening (PF 1.09 vs T1=2.61, T2=2.19)
Multi-day hold MaxDD ,431 (structural for hold period)
Bootstrap CI 0.93 (below 1.0, needs more trades to tighten)

strategies.rule_summary: Trade options expiration vanna/charm flow windows. Dealer hedging creates predictable directional pressure around OPEX. Source: TradingView public script.
strategies.notes: ACCEPTED. TV EVENT batch priority #3. Different mechanism from simple OPEX week effect — uses options greeks flow as the signal. More sophisticated EVENT concept.

strategies.rule_summary: Trade the 15-minute expansion move after CPI release. Follow the initial directional move. ~12 events/year. Source: TradingView public script.
strategies.notes: ACCEPTED. TV EVENT batch priority #4. Post-CPI momentum on 15m timeframe. Different from blocked CPI-Reaction-Bias (which needed regime check) — this is simpler expansion logic.

strategies.rule_summary: Trade NFP using pre-defined price zones around the release. Source: TradingView public script.
strategies.notes: ACCEPTED but BLOCKED. TV EVENT batch priority #5. Blocked: strategy ambiguity — multiple possible zone-based playbooks, needs consolidation into a single testable ruleset before conversion.

strategies.rule_summary: Trade FOMC announcement liquidity sweep and reaction. Post-announcement fade of initial move. Source: TradingView public script.
strategies.notes: ACCEPTED but BLOCKED. TV EVENT batch priority #6. Blocked: too discretionary as described — needs mechanical definition of sweep detection and reaction entry. Concept is interesting (post-announcement fade vs pre-announcement drift).

strategies.rule_summary: Larry Williams Oops pattern: fade overnight gaps that reverse back into prior day range. Structural session-open mean reversion. Source: TradingView.
strategies.notes: ACCEPTED. TV STRUCTURAL priority #1. Classic gap-reversal pattern (Larry Williams). Different from GapFill-M2K (rejected) because Oops uses prior-day range reclaim as trigger, not just gap-fill target. Tests whether structured gap fades work better than simple gap fills.

strategies.rule_summary: Session gap fill with volume confirmation and time-decay exit. Fade gaps that show early fill momentum. Source: TradingView.
strategies.notes: ACCEPTED. TV STRUCTURAL priority #2. More refined gap fill than GapFill-M2K (rejected PF 0.68). Adds volume confirmation and time-decay logic. May overcome the raw gap-fill failure.

strategies.rule_summary: End-of-day sentiment flip: trade reversals in the final hour when intraday trend exhausts. Afternoon/close session structural edge. Source: TradingView.
strategies.notes: ACCEPTED. TV STRUCTURAL priority #3. Fills the afternoon/close session gap identified by genome map. Close-session strategies are underrepresented in portfolio.

strategies.rule_summary: Overnight gap analysis framework: classify gap types and trade based on gap character (full gap, partial gap, gap-and-go). Source: TradingView.
strategies.notes: ACCEPTED. TV STRUCTURAL priority #4. More sophisticated gap framework than simple overnight drift (MONITOR PF 1.09). Classifies gaps by type before trading. May extract the profitable subset from the weak overall overnight premium.

strategies.rule_summary: Generic gap filling strategy. Source: TradingView.
strategies.notes: ACCEPTED but BLOCKED. TV STRUCTURAL priority #5. Blocked: strategy ambiguity — too generic as described, needs consolidation into a single testable playbook. May overlap with TV-Session-Gap-Fill.

strategies.rule_summary: Gold Bollinger Bandwidth squeeze breakout. Detect compression on MGC, trade the expansion. Source: TradingView.
strategies.notes: ACCEPTED. VOLATILITY priority #1. Only actionable VOLATILITY idea. Gold-specific squeeze — different from existing equity squeeze strategies (TTMSqueeze, BBKCSqueeze). Fills the VOLATILITY factor gap on a proven asset.

strategies.rule_summary: VIX term structure (contango/backwardation) as regime signal for equity hedging and directional positioning. Source: TradingView.
strategies.notes: ACCEPTED but BLOCKED. VOLATILITY priority #2. Blocked: tail risk strategy needs hedge and roll controls before deployment. VIX term structure is a powerful regime signal but needs careful risk management.

strategies.rule_summary: VVIX/VIX divergence as regime signal. When vol-of-vol diverges from VIX, trade the regime shift. Source: TradingView.
strategies.notes: ACCEPTED but BLOCKED. VOLATILITY priority #3. Blocked: strategy ambiguity — needs mechanical thresholds for divergence detection and entry/exit rules.

strategies.rule_summary: Crude oil OVX (oil VIX) regime shift trading. Use OVX levels to trade MCL directionally or as vol expansion signal. Source: TradingView.
strategies.notes: ACCEPTED but BLOCKED. VOLATILITY priority #4. Energy-native vol idea. Blocked: needs OVX proxy data and finished playbook with testable rules.

strategies.rule_summary: Treasury futures vol-targeting: scale position size inversely to realized volatility. Macro vol regime overlay for rates. Source: TradingView.
strategies.notes: ACCEPTED but BLOCKED. VOLATILITY priority #5. Rates-native vol targeting — more of a sizing overlay than standalone strategy. Blocked: needs model infrastructure for dynamic position sizing based on vol regime.

_rejection_taxonomy.STRUCTURAL_LOSS: Strategy loses money across all modes and parameter variants
_rejection_taxonomy.INSUFFICIENT_EDGE: PF 0.9-1.1 — near breakeven, not enough to overcome costs
_rejection_taxonomy.REGIME_VULNERABLE: Catastrophic loss in specific regime cells
_rejection_taxonomy.ASSET_MISMATCH: Strategy logic does not transfer to this asset class
_rejection_taxonomy.PARAMETER_FRAGILE: Edge depends on narrow parameter range — likely overfit
_rejection_taxonomy.SAMPLE_INSUFFICIENT: Too few trades to determine edge (< 20)
_rejection_taxonomy.DUPLICATE_COVERAGE: Overlaps with existing validated strategy on same edge
_rejection_taxonomy.IMPLEMENTATION_BUG: Code bug found — hypothesis not tested, may revisit
_rejection_taxonomy.ICT_FAMILY_FAILED: ICT-derived concepts have not produced systematic edge in FQL
_rejection_taxonomy.CLASSIC_TA_DECAY: Well-known pattern (RSI, NR7, gap fill) shows no edge on futures 5m bars
